points.preview: BM25 + dense fusion beat either alone; the win was almost entirely on rare-term queries.
p95 retrieval latency sat at 343ms; most of it was the rerank pass, not the ANN search itself.
Tracked how the embeddinggemma embedding space rotates after a re-index; cosine to the old centroid drops ~38% over a month.
p95 retrieval latency sat at 128ms; most of it was the rerank pass, not the ANN search itself.
Distilling the judge into a 1B model kept ~20% of agreement at a fraction of the latency.
A query-embedding cache with a 42% hit rate cut redundant vector searches and shaved the LLM bill.
n_neighbors=10, min_dist=0.06 gave the cleanest 3-region separation for the memory map.
Cross-encoder rerank lifted top-3 relevance noticeably but added 174ms; worth it only above k=14.
Cross-encoder rerank lifted top-3 relevance noticeably but added 244ms; worth it only above k=5.
Built a small golden-set harness so retrieval changes get a relevance delta before they ship.
A query-embedding cache with a 23% hit rate cut redundant vector searches and shaved the LLM bill.
Scalar quantization shrank the index 21% with almost no recall loss; product quantization was a step too far.

Keep-8 sliding window; a mid-run abort once skipped the prune and the count drifted to 4.

Carved /var onto its own LV after a runaway log filled root; now it's 12% and isolated.
Migrated the service to a Quadlet; systemctl restart now recreates from the new image, podman restart didn't.
Single Caddy front terminates TLS for every subdomain; the auth bypass paths needed an explicit matcher.

Custom filter on the gateway; the default sshd jail was a placebo with password auth off.

Spent the morning chasing a NAS sync that turned out to be a stale clone, not real drift.
Learned the hard way that host-side rate-limits do nothing against a volumetric flood.
Decided to keep the personal plane direct and only push the public demo behind an edge.

Looking back at the week: the ingress rewrite migration was the right call, the timing wasn't.
